items.title: Security Update for Adobe Commerce & Magento Open Source
items.summary: This update resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities that could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation. Adobe strongly recommends applying this security update to all eligible stores.

items.title: File Upload Vulnerability leading to Privilege Escalation
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.6) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to privilege escalation.

items.title: Improper Encoding or Escaping of Output leading to Arbitrary Code Execution
items.summary: A critical improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability (CVSS score: 9.1) in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source that could lead to arbitrary code execution.

items.title: Security Update for Adobe Commerce, Adobe Commerce B2B and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This bulletin fixes 15 CVEs, including critical Stored XSS (CVE-2026-34686, CVSS 8.7) leading to arbitrary code execution and Path Traversal (CVE-2026-34653, CVSS 8.7) leading to arbitrary file system write.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP object injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released a patched version (1.11.12) on May 25, 2026.

items.title: Critical Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: This critical security update addresses multiple vulnerabilities that could allow attackers to bypass security controls, gain escalated privileges, execute unauthorized malicious code, and gain unauthorized access to store data.

items.title: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files to any store using a polyglot technique to bypass validation. Adobe released Magento 2.4.9 with a fix, but older supported versions remain vulnerable.
